title: The BBC Cancels ‘Doctor Who’ Christmas Special

blocks.content: Make sure to do a good job gift wrapping your multicolored scarves, TARDIS socks, and Dalek-bedecked T-shirts, because that’s all the Whovian excitement available this Christmas. The BBC is not moving forward with its planned Doctor Who holiday special. “This decision was not taken lightly, and we know it will be disappointing for fans, but in order to set the show up for future series, it was decided that rather than bridge the gap with a one off special, we are choosing to push forward to invest in the long-term future of the show which ensures that when the TARDIS lands once more, it does so in all its glory,” the network said in a press release.

Current showrunner Russell T. Davies and Bad Wolf productions will no longer work on the series, and the network will look for new creative partners. “Doctor Who remains an important part of the BBC and this tender underpins the BBC’s continued commitment to Doctor Who, ensuring audiences will enjoy the show for years to come,” the network said. Davies originally worked on the show from 2005 to 2010, but he then returned from 2021 through 2025, casting Ncuti Gatwa as the first Black doctor. Now, Gatwa and Davies’s time on the show is over.

“And so GOODBYE from me to Doctor Who but HELLO to a big new future for the show, as the BBC announces it’s putting the show out to tender,” Davies wrote on Instagram. “As a result, there won’t be a Christmas Special - we only cooked that up to guarantee a future when no one knew what would happen, but now we do know, there’s no need for it. You’ll have to wait a bit longer for new Doctor Who… but you’ll be waiting for MORE Doctor Who than a one-off. So it’s worth it! For the record: there was no script, I never wrote it, and no actor was ever approached to play the next Doctor.” That means the two most iconic British roles of all time, James Bond and the Doctor, are currently up for grabs. Can Callum Turner play both?
